spec_match.rationale: Uses chromestatus motivation verbatim; demonstrates light-DOM container reachable from inside shadow root via flat tree.

interactive.rationale: Tree comparison with live container resolution.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Lede quotes the chromestatus rationale exactly.

edge_cases.rationale: Direct shadow-vs-flat contrast IS the edge case.

design_system.rationale: The displayed code sample and live shadow-root styles use --accent-blue and --bg-paper, with white-on-blue contrast in the matched state.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP on Google Chrome/Chromium 148 loaded http://localhost:3000/v130/css-container-queries-flat-tree-lookup/shadow-vs-flat-ancestry/, inspected the code sample showing var(--bg-paper), var(--accent-blue), and var(--bg-paper), filled the width slider to 650px and observed BLUE/query matched with background rgb(0, 34, 255) and text rgb(255, 255, 255), then filled it to 450px and observed WHITE/query did not match with black text. Console had no messages, and all network requests completed with page/styles/fonts 200 and favicon 204.
